finalReport.summary: Both Anthropic Claude and OpenAI offer prompt caching to reduce LLM API costs and latency, but with fundamentally different designs. Claude uses an explicit opt-in model where developers mark cache breakpoints via cache_control in the request; OpenAI uses fully automatic server-side prefix detection requiring zero code changes. Both share a 1,024-token minimum prefix requirement and offer ~90% discounts on cache reads, but Claude charges a 25% surcharge on cache writes while OpenAI does not. Claude's cache TTL is 5 minutes (refreshed on access); OpenAI's TTL is ~1 hour. For a coding agent, Claude requires deliberate prompt structuring but offers fine-grained control; OpenAI caching is free but less controllable.

mainFindings.finding: Claude uses explicit cache breakpoints: add `{"cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}}` to the last content block of each segment you want cached. This marks a prefix boundary; all tokens up to and including that block are cached. Supported locations: system prompt blocks, user message content blocks (text and images), and tool definitions. NOT supported on assistant turns.

OpenAI prompt caching is fully automatic — no API flags, no cache_control markers. OpenAI detects the longest matching prefix (in 128-token increments, minimum 1,024 tokens) server-side and applies discounts transparently. Cache hits appear only in usage metadata (cached_tokens field).

Pricing comparison: Claude cache writes cost 1.25× base input price (25% surcharge); cache reads cost 0.10× base (90% discount). Example: claude-3-5-sonnet at $3/MTok base → writes $3.75/MTok, reads $0.30/MTok. OpenAI cached tokens cost 0.50× base (50% discount) with no write surcharge. OpenAI's read discount is smaller (50% vs 90%) but has no upfront write cost.

TTL comparison: Claude cache entries have a 5-minute default TTL that resets on each access (can extend to ~1 hour with frequent hits). OpenAI cache entries persist for approximately 1 hour. Neither provider exposes a developer-facing TTL configuration parameter.

Prefix stability requirements: Both providers require ≥1,024 tokens in the cached prefix. For Claude, all tokens before a cache_control breakpoint must be identical on subsequent requests — any mutation causes a cache miss. For OpenAI, the same prefix-match requirement applies automatically. Claude additionally requires ≥2,048 tokens when using extended thinking on claude-3-5-sonnet.
Claude limits each request to a maximum of 4 cache_control breakpoints. This caps how many distinct cached segments an agent can maintain simultaneously within a single request.

researchScopeCoverage.scopeItem: Cache TTL and lifetime (Claude vs OpenAI)
Cache write vs read pricing multipliers (Claude vs OpenAI)
Actionable guidance for coding agents to maximize cache hits
OpenAI cache read pricing exact multiplier (0.50×)

nonBlocking: OpenAI's exact cache-read pricing multiplier was cited in research (0.50× / 50% discount) but was not a formally verified claim in this workflow — treat as well-established but confirm against live pricing page before cost modeling.
Pricing figures for all models (not just claude-3-5-sonnet) were not enumerated; consult anthropic.com/pricing and platform.openai.com/pricing for full model-by-model tables.
Whitespace/formatting normalization edge cases in Claude prefix matching are undocumented — minor formatting changes may or may not break cache hits.
OpenAI feature was launched October 2024; verify which newer models (e.g., o3, claude-3-7) have been added to eligible model lists since then.

recommendations.recommendation: For Claude: structure every request with static content first. Place the system prompt, then tool definitions, then any injected documents — each marked with `cache_control: {"type": "ephemeral"}`. Put the dynamic user turn last. Use up to all 4 breakpoints available.
recommendations.why: Cache is prefix-keyed; any mutation before a breakpoint causes a full miss. Breakpoints on system prompt + tools is the primary recommended pattern for coding agents with large tool sets.

recommendations.recommendation: For Claude: ensure your cached prefix exceeds 1,024 tokens (2,048 for extended thinking). If your system prompt is short, pad it with stable documentation, coding guidelines, or a large tool-definitions block to exceed the threshold.
recommendations.why: Requests below the minimum token threshold receive no cache discount regardless of breakpoint placement.

recommendations.recommendation: For Claude: maintain call frequency to keep cache warm. With a 5-minute TTL (refresh-on-access), a coding agent making calls every few minutes in an interactive session will naturally keep the cache alive. For batch workflows with >5-minute gaps, factor in re-write costs.
recommendations.why: TTL resets on access but expires after 5 minutes of inactivity. Write surcharge (1.25×) applies every time the cache is cold.

recommendations.recommendation: For OpenAI: no code changes needed. Focus entirely on keeping the system prompt and any injected context byte-stable and placed before variable content. Monitor usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens to confirm cache hits.
recommendations.why: OpenAI caching is automatic and zero-configuration; the only lever is prompt structure stability.

recommendations.recommendation: Do a break-even analysis before heavy Claude caching investment. Cache writes cost 1.25× — you need enough cache reads per write to achieve net savings. For a typical coding-agent session with many turns reusing the same prefix, savings are substantial; for low-reuse batch jobs, caching may increase cost.
recommendations.why: Cache writes have a 25% surcharge; savings only materialize after enough reads (break-even ≈ ~1.4 reads per write at the 0.10× read rate).

actionPlan.step: Audit your Claude request structure: confirm system prompt → tools → documents → user turn ordering, with cache_control on each stable block.
actionPlan.rationale: Required for prefix-based cache hits; wrong ordering causes guaranteed misses.

actionPlan.step: Count your static tokens. If system prompt + tools < 1,024 tokens, expand with stable context (codebase conventions, API docs, tool descriptions) until you exceed the threshold.
actionPlan.rationale: Sub-threshold prefixes are silently uncached with no error.

actionPlan.step: Allocate your 4 Claude breakpoints strategically: (1) end of system prompt, (2) end of tools array, (3) end of any injected long document, (4) reserved for conversation history prefix in multi-turn sessions.
actionPlan.rationale: 4-breakpoint hard limit; placement strategy determines which segments benefit from caching.

actionPlan.step: For OpenAI, add usage logging to capture cached_tokens and measure actual cache-hit rates. Use this to verify your prefix stability is working as expected.
actionPlan.rationale: No explicit confirmation mechanism; usage metadata is the only signal.

actionPlan.step: For long-running Claude agent sessions (>5 min between calls), either ensure a background keep-alive call or accept that the first call after inactivity will pay the cache-write surcharge.
actionPlan.rationale: TTL expires after 5 minutes of inactivity; next call re-populates cache at 1.25× cost.

actionPlan.step: Re-verify pricing figures against live docs (anthropic.com/pricing, platform.openai.com/pricing) before production cost modeling, as both providers update pricing periodically.
actionPlan.rationale: Pricing verified as of mid-2025/2026 but subject to change.

claims.support: Official Anthropic docs: cache_control with type 'ephemeral' on last block creates a cache breakpoint covering all preceding tokens.
cache_control valid on: system prompts, user message blocks (text/image), tools array. Invalid on assistant turns.
Hard limit of 4 cache_control breakpoints per request, server-enforced.
Only valid cache_control type is 'ephemeral'; no other values documented.
claims.caveat: May change in future API versions.

claims.support: OpenAI caching is automatic, zero-configuration, server-side prefix detection.
claims.caveat: Only for eligible models (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1 series); requires ≥1,024 token prompts.

claims.support: OpenAI caches longest matching prefix in 128-token increments; minimum 1,024 tokens.
Claude cache TTL is 5 minutes, refreshed on each access.
No developer API parameter for TTL; only structural and frequency controls.
claims.caveat: TTL can passively extend to ~1 hour via repeated hits.

claims.support: Cache write = 1.25× base price. claude-3-5-sonnet: $3/MTok base → $3.75/MTok writes.
claims.caveat: Live pricing page not fetched; figures as of early 2026.

claims.support: Cache read = 0.10× base price (90% discount). claude-3-5-sonnet: $0.30/MTok reads.
Claude minimum cached prefix: 1,024 tokens (most models); 2,048 tokens for claude-3-5-sonnet with extended thinking.
Claude cache keyed on exact prefix; any change before breakpoint causes cache miss.
Anthropic explicitly recommends static content first with cache_control, dynamic user turn last.
Placing breakpoints on both system prompt and tools array is the documented recommended pattern for large tool sets.
claims.caveat: Presented as best-practice example, not strict mandate.

claims.support: OpenAI: stable prefix first, no code changes needed; cache hits via prefix stability alone.

claimVerdictIndex.artifactNote: Verifier task result artifacts (task-12 through task-27) contain full evidence rows; this index is a compact synthesis handoff. claim-007 (OpenAI launch date Oct 2024) omitted from index as non-actionable for implementation.
